overview.short: Malhargad is a hill fort in western India near Saswad, 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Pune.
overview.description: Malhargad is a hill fort in western India near Saswad, 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Pune. It is also known as Sonori Fort due to the village of Sonori being situated at its base. The fort was named for the god Malhari and was the last fort built by the Marathas, in 1775. The Sahyadri range to the west of Pune in Velha taluka is split, and the forts Rajgad and Torna are on one branch while Forts Sinhagad, Purandar, Vajragad and Malhargad on the other.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Gurholi
topPlaces.description: Gurholi is a village in Purandar Taluka, India, with an agrarian economy, where fruits such as figs, custard apples, and pomegranates are produced. The village is surrounded by three mountains, and has temples among the mountains.

topPlaces.name: Fort Jadhavgadh
topPlaces.description: Fort Jadhavgarh is an 18th century fort, located in Pune, India. It was called Jadhavgadi, a mini fort built in 40 acre land three hundred years ago by Pilaji Jadhav a Maratha Lieutenant of Peshwa, Balaji Rao, in Pune, India In 2007, the Jadhav Family handed over the remains of Fort Jadhavgadh, a seven storey Mughal style structure with two underground, now spread over only 4.5 acres to Khil in 2007 for restoration and development.

topPlaces.name: Pimpale Khalsa
topPlaces.description: Pimpale Khalsa is a village in the Shirur taluka of Pune district in Maharashtra, India. Pimple Khalsa is other variation for the same name somewhere else.

topPlaces.name: Loni Kalbhor
topPlaces.description: Loni Kalbhor is a village 11 kilometres east of the City of Pune, Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Waghapur, Pune
topPlaces.description: Waghapur village is located in Purandar Tehsil of Pune district in Maharashtra, India. It is situated 15 km away from sub-district headquarter Saswad and 43 km away from district headquarter Pune.

topPlaces.name: Uruli Kanchan
topPlaces.description: Uruli Kanchan is a village 33 km east from the city of Pune in the district of Pune, Maharashtra, India. The village has been famous for the last sixty years for the Naturopathy Center (Nisarg Upchar Ashram) started by Mahatma Gandhi and his disciple Manibhai Desai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Malhargad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Malhargad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (23 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Malhargad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Malhargad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Malhargad?
faq.a: Stays in Malhargad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Malhargad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gurholi, Fort Jadhavgadh, Pimpale Khalsa, Loni Kalbhor, Waghapur, Pune.
